scene.description: The Null District. Read it one way and it's an abandoned city full of emotional traces. Read it the other and it's empty geometry with a corrected absence. The place doesn't change — only the rules you use to read it do.

fragments.label: Maya Reading — Traces
fragments.text: Through Maya's layer: homes mid-life. A kettle. A door left open. Every object still means something to someone.

fragments.label: Eden Reading — Structure
fragments.text: Through Eden's layer: clean geometry, the absence neatly corrected. No meaning to misread because no meaning is rendered.

fragments.label: Both At Once
fragments.text: Hold both layers together and they fight — emotional traces flickering over corrected geometry. The reconstruction can't decide which rules to obey.

fragments.label: The Null Core
fragments.symbol: At the district's center: NULL. Not empty — undefinable. A place that has no interpretation state at all, waiting for one to be assigned.

fragments.label: The Last Resident
fragments.text: One figure stands in the null, unbothered by being undefinable. They turn to you: 'It's quieter when nothing has to mean anything. You'll see.' You don't recognize them. You almost do.

fragments.label: The District Sign
fragments.text: A street sign at the edge, half-corrected by Eden, half-mourned by Maya. The district's name flickers between a word and a coordinate. It was someone's home and it was a data address. It was both.

interpretations.label: NULL is meaning removed faster than structure (Maya)
NULL is structure rejecting bad meaning (Eden)
NULL is reality awaiting an authority to define it
